title: They can feel us
wikitext:
[]: They can feel us ....             Quote Post by Lujerker » Tue Nov 07, 2006 8:40 pm Episode 1 .... Shinji in front of Gendou.... Then sachiel begin to attack Tokyo 3 ... gendou says.... "it's too near..... it can feel us..." Angels has 6th sense ? or it was for the critic discution moment between Shinji - Gendou ? Bye...

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Tue Nov 07, 2006 11:43 pm Angels appear to be able to communicate with others on a different level... it seems akin to psychic reading, or a spirit talk...not sure really id guess they communicate the way we would if it werent for AT Feilds Kaworu realises Lilith isn't Adam Arael mind rapes Asuka

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Wed Nov 08, 2006 12:07 pm That line is only in the dub, ie: it isn't true.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Nov 08, 2006 3:20 pm O RLY: IKARI: It must have detected our location.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 6:14 pm Feeling implies psychic phenomena, detecting can imply discovering through the use of tools such as reasoning, and sonar.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Nov 08, 2006 6:27 pm Reasoning? Ok :roll: ! Gaghiel apparently sensed mom on the "Over The Rainbow" any reason to think Sachiel didn't sense something composed of the same kind of matter as himself? Namely Yui (aka EVA-01) and/or Rei I (aka EVA-00). Both of which would seem mom like to him. Hell it's possible he sensed Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Nov 08, 2006 6:42 pm I'm not making claims how the angels detect things, I'm just pointing out that detect, and sense, are not necessarily the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Nov 08, 2006 6:43 pm Arael managed to tag Kyoko dead on from orbit so obviously they have some special senses we don't.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 08, 2006 8:13 pm Hasn't, like, Sachiel being zeroing in on "[their] location" for quite some time?

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Mon Nov 20, 2006 9:59 am They're probable more aware of the A.T. field than we are. Maybe they can sense the "Bad" guy's, or the enemy's, A.T. field.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Mon Nov 20, 2006 9:19 pm it could be how Asuka and Shinji communicated during 3-I...it may be like that...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 20, 2006 9:41 pm poetic_assassin wrote: They're probable more aware of the A.T. field than we are. Maybe they can sense the "Bad" guy's, or the enemy's, A.T. field.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Nov 20, 2006 10:02 pm Reichu wrote: poetic_assassin wrote: They're probable more aware of the A.T. field than we are. Maybe they can sense the "Bad" guy's, or the enemy's, A.T. field.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 20, 2006 10:36 pm

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Tue Nov 21, 2006 5:18 pm Well, could they not sense Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 21, 2006 5:22 pm Gaghiel unambiguously did. But ATFs had nothing to do with that case, since Adam didn't have one. (No soul.)

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Tue Nov 21, 2006 5:28 pm Ah, yes. Of course. But, angela have better fields than humans. So if it's anything like a morphogenetic field then my bet would be that they can 'feel' their enemies. Either this or there was a higher force directing them.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Nov 21, 2006 5:44 pm Reichu wrote: Gaghiel unambiguously did. But ATFs had nothing to do with that case, since Adam didn't have one. (No soul.)

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Tue Nov 21, 2006 5:48 pm Space Penis wrote: Reichu wrote: Gaghiel unambiguously did. But ATFs had nothing to do with that case, since Adam didn't have one. (No soul.)

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Tue Nov 21, 2006 6:05 pm poetic_assassin wrote: Space Penis wrote: Reichu wrote: Gaghiel unambiguously did. But ATFs had nothing to do with that case, since Adam didn't have one. (No soul.)
